title: Net-New Story Gap Review — 2026-05-16 (HRREC-82977)
content:
Executive summary (for PM)

  • Run tier: A (full contract). Gap Likelihood reflects Verdict + suggested missing BDD only—not a separate persona-tension score.
  • Scope: 44 Stories under HRREC-82977; skipped doc-writer keys HRREC-90852 (AG:) and HRREC-90853 (RN:).

Top 5 gaps (epic)

  • Notification routing (bell, My Conversations, optional email) still needs explicit India MVP defaults and per-user read semantics (HRREC-91975).
  • Admin channel toggle and conversation lifecycle stories pack policy, audit, and mid-session recruiter experience into adjacent tickets (HRREC-91978, HRREC-92012).
  • Identity-heavy slices (merge, primary email change, REST send failures) need recover-forward behaviour recruiters can trust (HRREC-92005, HRREC-92015, HRREC-92004).
  • Cross-surface audibility (Activity Stream, Timeline, Candidate Communications) must stay metadata- and attachment-aligned (HRREC-92035).
  • My Conversations refactor remains a thin placeholder without DoR-backed acceptance (HRREC-92022).

Top 5 strengths (epic)

  • Broad scenario coverage across compose, validation, discard, thread reading, agency isolation, and delivery states.
  • Explicit UI-only vs send-path separation for RTE, attachments, and styling stories reduces scope creep.
  • Privacy purge story names PDT objects to remove, improving GDPR conversations.
  • Initialisation stories anchor SSP ordering, masking, and panel growth with Figma-first language.
  • Slack archive surfaced programme context (SES, volume, security reviews) for attachment and delivery risk discussions.

Epic-level notes

  • Overlap: Compose, To/From, attachments, send, and discard stories interlock; sequencing refinements should call out which story owns cross-cutting error copy.
  • Agency vs non-agency: Tabs, compose eligibility, and To-list stories should share one recruiter-facing mental model to avoid contradictory opt-in timing.
  • Reporting: HRREC-92013 exploratory pass should consume the same logging assumptions as HRREC-92035 to avoid duplicate QE effort.
  • XO adjacency: Several Dev lens cells show generic SUV metadata hits; plan a short engineering readout to map UI stories to concrete XO layouts and REST views.

Cross-initiative pattern hints (WhatsApp — inspiration only)

Hard disclaimers (verbatim intent): (1) Same surface, different channel: consent, templates, media, delivery semantics, and failure modes differ. (2) Different backend / OE: WhatsApp partner delivery is often Twilio-oriented; two-way email uses Workday’s email path—do not assume parity. (3) Bugs under WhatsApp epics signal delivery pain, not proof of email defects. (4) Inspiration only—phrase as “Consider for 2WE if…”, never “2WE must match WhatsApp.”

Frozen pattern corpus — snapshot_as_of 2026-05-16 in docs/initiatives/two-way-email/reference/WHATSAPP_COMPANION_CORPUS_SNAPSHOT.md; manifest_complete: true; No live Jira pull for WhatsApp companion this run (manifest-only).

  • HRREC-84403 / HRREC-89856 — Preview vs send hydration and security context: ask whether email Message Builder previews use the same resolved fields the candidate ultimately receives.
  • HRREC-89979 — Avoid “silent until send” when limits are hit; surface early when opening the email task if a conversation cannot be created.
  • HRREC-90243 — Lifecycle banners should appear at the business event (decline, hire, close), not only after first outbound.
  • HRREC-90306 / HRREC-84389 — After merge, confirm email identity and threading keys follow the surviving candidate, not a merged shadow record.
  • HRREC-84556 — Reporting and CRF naming: plan email fields so mixed-channel dashboards stay interpretable.
Story Gap Likelihood PM lens QA lens Dev lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)
HRREC-91946
[Initialisation] Recruiter can see Email Task on SSP (XO Only)		LowGreen

Minor clarifier; one narrow BDD.

  • Jira — Email task in sliding side panel ordering after notes before SMS; masked job app handling; icon/tooltip per Figma; HRI for new-item count.
  • Salomon — SSP/collaboration-panel rules for task ordering; WhatsApp-style gating when setup missing.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: Peers HRREC-91974 empty state, HRREC-91985 compose, HRREC-92008 agency visibility.
  • Salomon — No archived Slack hits for the sampled query on this key.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — Conversation Context Instance class; WATS on collaboration panel candidate views.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Initialisation is comparatively bounded; residual risk is masked job applications interacting with panel grow rules.
  • Recommended next step: Run one joint UX plus QE pass on masked requisitions with grow/shrink breakpoints before freeze.

Scenario: Masked application icon behaviour

Given a candidate has a masked job application configured in tenant maintenance
When a recruiter opens the profile sliding panel
Then the email task icon and ordering follow Figma relative to notes and SMS without duplicate masked icons

  • Salomon — SSP expanded state persistence vs UI Platform default narrow side panels.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: HRREC-90236 sliding panel CK; HRREC-91980/91982/92001 panel interactions.
  • Salomon — No archived Slack hits for the sampled query on this key.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — Collaboration Panel WATS visibility with/without access on candidate and job application.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Initialisation is comparatively bounded; residual risk is masked job applications interacting with panel grow rules.
  • Recommended next step: Run one joint UX plus QE pass on masked requisitions with grow/shrink breakpoints before freeze.

Scenario: Masked application icon behaviour

Given a candidate has a masked job application configured in tenant maintenance
When a recruiter opens the profile sliding panel
Then the email task icon and ordering follow Figma relative to notes and SMS without duplicate masked icons

HRREC-91975
PH: Recruiter receives a notification		MediumBlue

Routing follow-up; one BDD.

  • Jira — Inbound reply fires framework notification; My Conversations with bell fallback; optional email alerts Candidate SMS/Email Messages type.
  • Salomon — SMS conversational notifications bell and email routing types; Admin Guide My Conversations SMS-centric.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: HRREC-89871 custom notifications; HRREC-92011 purge; HRREC-90657 amend.
  • Salomon — No archived Slack hits for the sampled query on this key.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — My Conversations notification search errored; Candidate SMS Conversations WATS as proxy for eligibility.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Notification routing spans framework events, My Conversations, bell, and optional email alerts—Deployment Agent could not confirm India MVP defaults.
  • Recommended next step: Confirm notification type names, default routes, and per-user read semantics with engineering before copy freeze.

Scenario: Inbound reply notification surfaces

Given two recruiters share access to the same candidate conversation
When an inbound reply is ingested while one recruiter already cleared unread
Then bell and My Conversations behaviours follow the per-user vs shared read model documented in Jira

HRREC-91978
PH: Admin can enable or disable 2-way email as a comms channel		HighYellow

Toggle mid-flight gap; one BDD.

  • Jira — Tenant admin toggle; disable mid-flight blocks new sends; auditable record on change.
  • Salomon — Feature toggle XO articles; little crisp admin switch precedent for this channel.
  • Deployment Agent — Tenant email and recruiting messaging behaviour is usually shaped under Edit Tenant Setup - Recruiting plus security on Candidate Communication and related sharing domains; the agent could not pull Jira-specific runbooks for HRREC-82977, so validate your sandbox has the same domains and notification types your scenarios assume.
  • Jira — Stress permissions, mid-session state changes, and whether errors stay on the email task versus generic page banners.
  • Salomon — Related HRREC signals: JQL noisy legacy conversion and WhatsApp items.
  • Salomon — No archived Slack hits for the sampled query on this key.
  • Deployment Agent — Inbound signals often land as bell notifications when routing types and security groups align; the agent noted My Conversations wording may differ by product area—confirm with engineering which surface your notification story must light up in for India MVP tenants.
  • XO MCP — Recruiting email targeted search low signal on SUV snapshot.
  • Peanut — Not queried — no trigger
  • Finding: Channel enable/disable must stay coherent for recruiters mid-session and leave an auditable admin trail without surprise loss of drafts.
  • Recommended next step: Workshop toggle behaviour with OE: what happens to in-flight compose, queued sends, and thread visibility when the channel flips off.

Scenario: Admin disables channel during active compose

Given a recruiter has an unsent draft open on the email task
When a tenant administrator disables two-way email while that session is still active
Then new sends are blocked with the agreed copy, prior threads remain readable per policy, and the recruiter is not left with a silent non-save
